title: allocarya chorisiana var. undulata
intro: Allocarya chorisiana var. undulata, also known as the undulate allocarya or desert broom, is a flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant is a shrub that belongs to the carrot family and is known for its attractive, white or yellow flowers and fern-like foliage. Traditionally, Allocarya chorisiana var. undulata has been used in herbal medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help to soothe and calm skin irritations and wounds. The plant can be prepared as a tea or tincture, and its health benefits are said to include reducing fever, promoting relaxation, and supporting the immune system.
